statement.inputFormat:
Input format: Two lines, first line with the size of the array and second line with elements separated by spaces.

Example:

```
5
1 3 5 7 9
```
statement.outputFormat: Return `true` if the target exists in the array, otherwise `false`.
statement.notes: The array is sorted in ascending order.

hints.text: The binary search algorithm starts by comparing the target to the middle element in the array.

editorial.title: Editorial Title
editorial.intuition: To fix the incorrect early return, we need to adjust the condition inside the if statement in the binary search logic.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute force approach would be to linearly search for the target element in the array, which has a time complexity of O(n).
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution uses binary search with a time complexity of O(log n), making it more efficient for large inputs.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness for the binary search algorithm can be found in many computer science textbooks.
editorial.dryRun: Performing a dry run by hand is a useful technique to see how the algorithm works and how different cases should be handled.
commonMistakes: Incorrectly returning `true` when the target is smaller than the smallest element in the array.
